compiled_state.title: test-batched.sh treats interrupted tests as completed on resume, preventing re-run
compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body:
Bug: test-batched.sh records a test as 'interrupted' when it hits the time boundary, and on resume it skips the test because it's 'already completed'. The state file marks interrupted tests the same as completed tests, preventing re-run.

Reproduction:
1. Run test-batched.sh --timeout=60 with a test that takes >60s
2. Script records test as interrupted in state file
3. Re-run with the NEXT: command — script skips the test as 'already completed'
4. Reports '1 interrupted' but '0 passed, 0 failed'

Session evidence:
- rm -f state file + validate.sh --ci → tests PENDING (first run times out)
- validate.sh --ci (second run) → FAIL with '1 interrupted'
- test-batched.sh resume → 'Skipping (already completed)' — never actually re-runs

Expected: interrupted tests should be retried on resume, not skipped.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body: Root cause confirmed: same as w22-r5fx which was already fixed. plugins/dso/scripts/test-batched.sh line 331 now filters interrupted tests from COMPLETED_LIST on resume so they are re-run. Closing as duplicate/already fixed.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart
